forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: c680
+title: 'Bug: Cannot launch command palette тАФ not in menu, not by hotkey'
+---
+## What
+Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened тАФ neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut.
+
+The palette opener command is `ui.palette.open` (cua `Mod+K`, vim `:`) тАФ see `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts:864,1062`. The palette overlay itself is `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`, rendered in `app-shell.tsx` gated on `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671,818). Backend state is `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` (`apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1831`).
+
+Two distinct failure surfaces to investigate:
+1. **Hotkey path**: the keybindings tests note `Mod+K`/`:` only fire "when a scope claims it" (keybindings.test.ts:1062тАУ1097). If no focused scope surfaces `ui.palette.open`, the binding never resolves. Determine which scope is expected to claim `ui.palette.open` and why it isn't at runtime.
+2. **Menu path**: `ui.palette.open` appears to have no `menu` placement, so `collect_menu_entries` (`menu.rs:88`) never adds it to any native submenu тАФ there is no menu affordance at all. Decide the correct menu home (likely View or App) and add the placement, OR confirm the intended affordance.
+
+NOTE: likely shares a root cause with the blank-Navigation-menu bug and the jump-to-inspect bug тАФ focus/command surfacing at runtime. Cross-check before fixing in isolation.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `Mod+K` (cua) / `:` (vim) opens the command palette from a normal board focus.
+- [ ] There is a discoverable native-menu affordance that opens the palette.
+- [ ] Root cause documented (scope not claiming the command vs. binding resolution vs. missing menu placement).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` to assert `Mod+K`/`:` dispatches `ui.palette.open` under the real board scope chain (not a synthetic single-scope fixture).
+- [ ] If a menu placement is added: a `menu.rs` test asserting `ui.palette.open` collects into its intended submenu key.
+- [ ] Regression test failing before, passing after.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` тАФ failing test first, then fix.
\ No newline at end of file

-1. **Hotkey path**: the keybindings tests note `Mod+K`/`:` only fire "when a scope claims it" (keybindings.test.ts:1062тАУ1097). If no focused scope surfaces `ui.palette.open`, the binding never resolves. Determine which scope is expected to claim `ui.palette.open` and why it isn't at runtime.
-2. **Menu path**: `ui.palette.open` appears to have no `menu` placement, so `collect_menu_entries` (`menu.rs:88`) never adds it to any native submenu тАФ there is no menu affordance at all. Decide the correct menu home (likely View or App) and add the placement, OR confirm the intended affordance.

-- [ ] If a menu placement is added: a `menu.rs` test asserting `ui.palette.open` collects into its intended submenu key.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,25 +6,41 @@
 title: 'Bug: Cannot launch command palette тАФ not in menu, not by hotkey'
 ---
 ## What
-Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened тАФ neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut.
+Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened тАФ neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut. (User pressed a hotkey and nothing happened.)
+
+## It is NOT a window тАФ it's an overlay React component
+`<CommandPalette>` is rendered in `app-shell.tsx:818-826` as `<FocusLayer name="palette"><CommandPalette/></FocusLayer>`, **portaled to `document.body`** (`createPortal`, see comment app-shell.tsx:741-746). It is conditionally mounted only when `paletteOpen` is true. `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671) тАФ the **per-window backend UIState flag**. So the open path is: command тЖТ backend sets `palette_open=true` тЖТ `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` тЖТ `ui-state-context` updates `windows[label].palette_open` тЖТ AppShell re-renders тЖТ overlay mounts. No OS window involved.
+
+## Root cause (investigated): three-way divergence in the open path + no menu entry
+There are **three** palette-open command IDs, all wired to backend handlers, but the frontend keybindings only listen for one of them:
+
+| Command ID | Backend handler (`commands/mod.rs`) | Declared keys | Frontend listens? |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `ui.palette.open` | `PaletteOpenCmd` (mod.rs:150) | `ui.yaml`: cua **Mod+K**, vim `:` | тЭМ NOT in `BINDING_TABLES`, NOT in any frontend scope |
+| `app.command` | `CommandPaletteCmd` (mod.rs:310) | `app.yaml`: vim `:`, cua/emacs **Mod+Shift+P** | тЬЕ vim `:` тЖТ `app.command` |
+| `app.palette` | `CommandPaletteCmd` alias (mod.rs:315) | **Mod+Shift+P** all modes | тЬЕ `Mod+Shift+P` тЖТ `app.palette` |

-The palette opener command is `ui.palette.open` (cua `Mod+K`, vim `:`) тАФ see `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts:864,1062`. The palette overlay itself is `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`, rendered in `app-shell.tsx` gated on `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671,818). Backend state is `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` (`apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1831`).
+- Frontend static `BINDING_TABLES` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts:27-140`) only bind `Mod+Shift+P тЖТ app.palette` (all modes) and vim `:` тЖТ `app.command`.
+- The frontend global command set (`STATIC_GLOBAL_COMMANDS`, app-shell.tsx:170-180) contains `app.command` and `app.palette` тАФ but **NOT `ui.palette.open`**. So `extractScopeBindings` never surfaces `ui.palette.open`, and `Mod+K` is not in `BINDING_TABLES` тЖТ **`Mod+K` resolves to nothing in the live app.**
+- No palette command (`ui.palette.open` / `app.command` / `app.palette`) carries a `menu:` placement тЖТ **no native menu affordance at all** (matches "not on the menu").

-Two distinct failure surfaces to investigate:
-1. **Hotkey path**: the keybindings tests note `Mod+K`/`:` only fire "when a scope claims it" (keybindings.test.ts:1062тАУ1097). If no focused scope surfaces `ui.palette.open`, the binding never resolves. Determine which scope is expected to claim `ui.palette.open` and why it isn't at runtime.
-2. **Menu path**: `ui.palette.open` appears to have no `menu` placement, so `collect_menu_entries` (`menu.rs:88`) never adds it to any native submenu тАФ there is no menu affordance at all. Decide the correct menu home (likely View or App) and add the placement, OR confirm the intended affordance.
+**Conclusion:** the registry/menu world advertises `ui.palette.open` / `Mod+K`, but the running frontend only opens the palette via **`Mod+Shift+P`** (or vim `:`). The user almost certainly pressed the advertised `Mod+K`, which is dead on the frontend.

-NOTE: likely shares a root cause with the blank-Navigation-menu bug and the jump-to-inspect bug тАФ focus/command surfacing at runtime. Cross-check before fixing in isolation.
+## First implementer step тАФ bisect A vs B
+1. **A (most likely):** `Mod+Shift+P` DOES open the palette тЖТ the bug is the keybinding/menu divergence. Fix by consolidating to ONE palette command (prefer `ui.palette.open`), wiring its key into `BINDING_TABLES` / the frontend global scope, deleting the `app.command`/`app.palette` duplication, and adding a `menu:` placement (View or App) so there's a clickable affordance.
+2. **B (deeper):** if `Mod+Shift+P` ALSO does nothing, the failure is downstream тАФ `palette_open` not propagating (`ui-state-context`), the `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` envelope not unwrapping (cf. the `{ok,change}` envelope issue in recent commit af9e6e965), or the `<FocusLayer parentLayerFq>` mount being rejected. Capture `log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"'` while pressing the key.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `Mod+K` (cua) / `:` (vim) opens the command palette from a normal board focus.
+- [ ] A single documented shortcut opens the palette in all keymap modes, and it matches what the registry/menu advertise (no Mod+K vs Mod+Shift+P divergence).
 - [ ] There is a discoverable native-menu affordance that opens the palette.
-- [ ] Root cause documented (scope not claiming the command vs. binding resolution vs. missing menu placement).
+- [ ] The three-way `ui.palette.open` / `app.command` / `app.palette` duplication is reconciled to a single source of truth (or intentional aliases that all share one binding wired into the frontend).
+- [ ] Root cause confirmed as A or B (see bisect step) and documented.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` to assert `Mod+K`/`:` dispatches `ui.palette.open` under the real board scope chain (not a synthetic single-scope fixture).
-- [ ] If a menu placement is added: a `menu.rs` test asserting `ui.palette.open` collects into its intended submenu key.
-- [ ] Regression test failing before, passing after.
+- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` so the canonical palette shortcut resolves to the canonical palette command under the real board scope chain.
+- [ ] A `menu.rs` test asserting the palette command collects into its intended submenu key (once a `menu:` placement is added).
+- [ ] If B: a `ui-state-context` test that a `PaletteOpen` change flips `windows[label].palette_open` and mounts the overlay.
+- [ ] Regression test failing before the fix, passing after.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` тАФ failing test first, then fix. #bug
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` тАФ failing test first, then fix.
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -6,41 +6,25 @@
 title: 'Bug: Cannot launch command palette тАФ not in menu, not by hotkey'
 ---
 ## What
-Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened тАФ neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut. (User pressed a hotkey and nothing happened.)
+Reported by user: the command palette cannot be opened тАФ neither from the native menu nor by a keyboard shortcut.

-## It is NOT a window тАФ it's an overlay React component
-`<CommandPalette>` is rendered in `app-shell.tsx:818-826` as `<FocusLayer name="palette"><CommandPalette/></FocusLayer>`, **portaled to `document.body`** (`createPortal`, see comment app-shell.tsx:741-746). It is conditionally mounted only when `paletteOpen` is true. `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671) тАФ the **per-window backend UIState flag**. So the open path is: command тЖТ backend sets `palette_open=true` тЖТ `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` тЖТ `ui-state-context` updates `windows[label].palette_open` тЖТ AppShell re-renders тЖТ overlay mounts. No OS window involved.
+The palette opener command is `ui.palette.open` (cua `Mod+K`, vim `:`) тАФ see `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts:864,1062`. The palette overlay itself is `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx`, rendered in `app-shell.tsx` gated on `paletteOpen = winState?.palette_open` (app-shell.tsx:671,818). Backend state is `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` (`apps/kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1831`).

-## Root cause (investigated): three-way divergence in the open path + no menu entry
-There are **three** palette-open command IDs, all wired to backend handlers, but the frontend keybindings only listen for one of them:
+Two distinct failure surfaces to investigate:
+1. **Hotkey path**: the keybindings tests note `Mod+K`/`:` only fire "when a scope claims it" (keybindings.test.ts:1062тАУ1097). If no focused scope surfaces `ui.palette.open`, the binding never resolves. Determine which scope is expected to claim `ui.palette.open` and why it isn't at runtime.
+2. **Menu path**: `ui.palette.open` appears to have no `menu` placement, so `collect_menu_entries` (`menu.rs:88`) never adds it to any native submenu тАФ there is no menu affordance at all. Decide the correct menu home (likely View or App) and add the placement, OR confirm the intended affordance.

-| Command ID | Backend handler (`commands/mod.rs`) | Declared keys | Frontend listens? |
-|---|---|---|---|
-| `ui.palette.open` | `PaletteOpenCmd` (mod.rs:150) | `ui.yaml`: cua **Mod+K**, vim `:` | тЭМ NOT in `BINDING_TABLES`, NOT in any frontend scope |
-| `app.command` | `CommandPaletteCmd` (mod.rs:310) | `app.yaml`: vim `:`, cua/emacs **Mod+Shift+P** | тЬЕ vim `:` тЖТ `app.command` |
-| `app.palette` | `CommandPaletteCmd` alias (mod.rs:315) | **Mod+Shift+P** all modes | тЬЕ `Mod+Shift+P` тЖТ `app.palette` |
-
-- Frontend static `BINDING_TABLES` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.ts:27-140`) only bind `Mod+Shift+P тЖТ app.palette` (all modes) and vim `:` тЖТ `app.command`.
-- The frontend global command set (`STATIC_GLOBAL_COMMANDS`, app-shell.tsx:170-180) contains `app.command` and `app.palette` тАФ but **NOT `ui.palette.open`**. So `extractScopeBindings` never surfaces `ui.palette.open`, and `Mod+K` is not in `BINDING_TABLES` тЖТ **`Mod+K` resolves to nothing in the live app.**
-- No palette command (`ui.palette.open` / `app.command` / `app.palette`) carries a `menu:` placement тЖТ **no native menu affordance at all** (matches "not on the menu").
+NOTE: likely shares a root cause with the blank-Navigation-menu bug and the jump-to-inspect bug тАФ focus/command surfacing at runtime. Cross-check before fixing in isolation.

-**Conclusion:** the registry/menu world advertises `ui.palette.open` / `Mod+K`, but the running frontend only opens the palette via **`Mod+Shift+P`** (or vim `:`). The user almost certainly pressed the advertised `Mod+K`, which is dead on the frontend.
-
-## First implementer step тАФ bisect A vs B
-1. **A (most likely):** `Mod+Shift+P` DOES open the palette тЖТ the bug is the keybinding/menu divergence. Fix by consolidating to ONE palette command (prefer `ui.palette.open`), wiring its key into `BINDING_TABLES` / the frontend global scope, deleting the `app.command`/`app.palette` duplication, and adding a `menu:` placement (View or App) so there's a clickable affordance.
-2. **B (deeper):** if `Mod+Shift+P` ALSO does nothing, the failure is downstream тАФ `palette_open` not propagating (`ui-state-context`), the `UIStateChange::PaletteOpen` envelope not unwrapping (cf. the `{ok,change}` envelope issue in recent commit af9e6e965), or the `<FocusLayer parentLayerFq>` mount being rejected. Capture `log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"'` while pressing the key.
-
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] A single documented shortcut opens the palette in all keymap modes, and it matches what the registry/menu advertise (no Mod+K vs Mod+Shift+P divergence).
+- [ ] `Mod+K` (cua) / `:` (vim) opens the command palette from a normal board focus.
 - [ ] There is a discoverable native-menu affordance that opens the palette.
-- [ ] The three-way `ui.palette.open` / `app.command` / `app.palette` duplication is reconciled to a single source of truth (or intentional aliases that all share one binding wired into the frontend).
-- [ ] Root cause confirmed as A or B (see bisect step) and documented.
+- [ ] Root cause documented (scope not claiming the command vs. binding resolution vs. missing menu placement).

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` so the canonical palette shortcut resolves to the canonical palette command under the real board scope chain.
-- [ ] A `menu.rs` test asserting the palette command collects into its intended submenu key (once a `menu:` placement is added).
-- [ ] If B: a `ui-state-context` test that a `PaletteOpen` change flips `windows[label].palette_open` and mounts the overlay.
-- [ ] Regression test failing before the fix, passing after.
+- [ ] Frontend: extend `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/lib/keybindings.test.ts` to assert `Mod+K`/`:` dispatches `ui.palette.open` under the real board scope chain (not a synthetic single-scope fixture).
+- [ ] If a menu placement is added: a `menu.rs` test asserting `ui.palette.open` collects into its intended submenu key.
+- [ ] Regression test failing before, passing after.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` тАФ failing test first, then fix.
